name: Starsky Robotics
summary: Autonomous trucking startup that pursued driverless highway trucking with a remote-teleoperation backup, but shut down in 2020 after failing to raise a new funding round.
lesson: Deep-tech capital-intensive bets need a much longer runway plan than typical SaaS fundraising cycles allow.

name: Embark Trucks
summary: Public autonomous-trucking company (SPAC-listed 2021) that laid off most of its staff and sold its assets/IP in 2023 after burning through cash without a path to commercial deployment.
lesson: Going public via SPAC does not solve an unresolved commercialization timeline for hard technology.

name: TuSimple
summary: Autonomous-trucking company that went public on Nasdaq in 2021 but unraveled amid governance scandals, an SEC/DOJ investigation, and a pivot to China, winding down its US trucking operations by 2024.
lesson: Founder and board governance failures can destroy a technically promising company even with a public listing and billions raised.

name: Ike Robotics
summary: Autonomous-trucking startup founded by ex-Google/Uber engineers that was acquired by and folded into Nuro in 2022, effectively ending its independent trucking ambitions.
lesson: A narrow talent-driven acquihire is often the real outcome when a capital-intensive autonomy startup can't reach scale alone.

name: 6 River Systems
summary: Warehouse collaborative-robotics maker acquired by Shopify in 2019 for ~$450M; Shopify shut the unit down in 2022 as it retreated from in-house logistics/fulfillment infrastructure.
lesson: Being acquired is not a permanent exit — a parent's strategic pivot can unwind an otherwise successful business unit.

name: Berkshire Grey
summary: Warehouse robotics and automation company that went public via SPAC in 2021, then saw its stock collapse and was acquired for parts by SoftBank in 2024 at a steep discount to its raised capital.
lesson: SPAC listings can mask unit-economics problems in hardware businesses that eventually surface in the public markets.

name: Attabotics
summary: Canadian 3D warehouse robotics/goods-to-person storage startup that ceased operations and filed for insolvency in 2024 after failing to secure additional financing.
lesson: Novel warehouse hardware architectures require sustained capital far beyond initial pilots to reach profitable scale.

name: inVia Robotics
summary: Warehouse robotics-as-a-service company providing autonomous mobile robots for e-commerce fulfillment that shut down in 2024 after running out of funding.
lesson: Robotics-as-a-service pricing must cover the true capital cost of hardware deployment or margins erode with scale.

name: Takeoff Technologies
summary: Micro-fulfillment center technology provider for grocery retailers that filed for bankruptcy in 2023 after major partners scaled back automated grocery fulfillment investments.
lesson: A vendor's fate is tightly coupled to customer capex cycles when the business model depends on partners building expensive infrastructure.

name: Fabric (CommonSense Robotics)
summary: Micro-fulfillment robotics company that raised huge rounds at a unicorn valuation but underwent mass layoffs and effective collapse in 2023 amid a shrinking market for automated micro-fulfillment.
lesson: Hype-driven mega-rounds can push valuations far ahead of real commercial traction in capital-intensive automation.
source: Business Insider, Fabric layoffs and collapse 2023

name: Ohi
summary: Distributed micro-fulfillment startup for D2C brands that shut down in 2023 after failing to reach sustainable unit economics in a soft e-commerce funding environment.
lesson: Asset-light distributed fulfillment networks still carry heavy fixed costs that punish thin margins.

name: Deliv
summary: Crowdsourced same-day last-mile delivery company partnered with major retailers that shut down in 2020 after losing ground to Amazon, DoorDash, and Uber's logistics expansion.
lesson: Last-mile delivery marketplaces need durable differentiation once tech giants enter with subsidized scale.

name: Shyp
summary: On-demand shipping and pickup startup that shut down in 2018 after failing to make the underlying pickup-and-pack logistics economically viable at consumer price points.
lesson: Consumer convenience services layered on top of expensive physical logistics rarely clear a sustainable margin.

name: Emerge
summary: Digital freight marketplace startup that abruptly ceased operations in 2023 amid allegations of financial misrepresentation to investors and lenders.
lesson: Freight marketplaces built on opaque financial reporting collapse quickly once lenders and investors lose trust.
source: FreightWaves, Emerge shutdown and fraud allegations 2023

name: Quiqup
summary: UK-based on-demand delivery and logistics startup serving retailers that collapsed into administration in 2021 after failing to reach profitability.
lesson: Subscale last-mile delivery networks struggle to compete once larger platforms achieve density-driven cost advantages.

name: Fetchr
summary: Middle East last-mile delivery and address-geolocation startup that collapsed in 2020 amid unpaid wages, investor lawsuits, and allegations of financial mismanagement.
lesson: Rapid regional expansion without financial controls can mask a business that is not actually solvent.

name: Haven
funding: Backed by Maersk and McKinsey Ventures
summary: Ocean freight booking and supply-chain visibility joint venture backed by Maersk and McKinsey that was shut down in 2020 as Maersk consolidated digital efforts internally.
lesson: Corporate-backed joint ventures can be wound down quickly once the parent's strategic priorities shift internally.

name: Boxbee
summary: On-demand storage and pickup logistics startup that shut down in 2016 after failing to scale its physical storage-and-delivery network profitably.
lesson: Physical-asset-heavy on-demand services need dense, repeatable routes to make unit economics work.

name: Transfix
summary: Digital freight brokerage marketplace that ceased its freight brokerage operations in 2023, selling off remaining technology assets after failing to achieve profitable scale.
lesson: Asset-light freight marketplaces face thin margins that require enormous volume to offset fixed technology and sales costs.

name: E2open
summary: Public supply-chain management software company whose stock collapsed over 90% after a 2024 accounting scandal involving misrepresented sales guidance and subsequent executive departures and shareholder litigation.
lesson: Aggressive roll-up acquisition strategies can hide accounting problems that eventually surface and destroy shareholder value.
source: SEC filings and Reuters, E2open accounting scandal 2024

name: Doorman
summary: Last-mile delivery scheduling startup letting consumers pick evening delivery windows that shut down in 2019 after failing to reach sustainable scale.
lesson: Consumer-facing delivery scheduling layers struggle without owning enough of the underlying delivery economics.

name: Parcelly
summary: UK click-and-collect parcel locker network startup that shut down in 2018 after struggling to build a large enough retail pickup-point network to be viable.
lesson: Network-effect logistics businesses need critical mass of physical pickup points before they become useful to consumers.

name: Elementum
summary: Supply-chain visibility and risk-management software platform backed by Flex that quietly wound down operations around 2020 after failing to differentiate against larger visibility platforms.
lesson: Being spun out of a large strategic backer does not guarantee independent commercial traction in a crowded category.
source: Industry reporting on supply-chain visibility consolidation, 2020

name: Convey
summary: Post-purchase delivery experience and last-mile exception-management software company that wound down in 2023, with some technology assets acquired by FedEx.
lesson: Middleware layers on top of carrier data can be squeezed out once carriers build the same visibility features natively.
source: FreightWaves, Convey wind-down and FedEx asset deal 2023

name: Peloton Technology
summary: Truck platooning technology company using vehicle-to-vehicle connectivity to reduce fuel costs that shut down in 2023 after failing to achieve commercial adoption at scale.
lesson: Technologies requiring fleet-wide coordinated adoption face a chicken-and-egg problem that can stall commercialization indefinitely.

name: Flirtey
summary: Drone delivery startup that conducted early landmark FAA-approved delivery trials but ceased meaningful operations around 2022 after failing to reach commercial scale ahead of larger, better-funded competitors.
lesson: Being first to demonstrate a technology publicly does not guarantee winning the commercialization race against better-capitalized rivals.

name: Instafreight
summary: German digital road-freight forwarder that merged into rival Forto in 2022 after failing to independently reach profitability amid intense competition among European freight-tech startups.
lesson: When multiple well-funded competitors chase the same freight-tech niche, consolidation often follows the funding downturn.

name: Forto
summary: German digital freight forwarder (formerly FreightHub) that cut roughly half its workforce and restructured operations in 2023 after aggressive expansion outpaced sustainable revenue.
lesson: Rapid geographic expansion in freight forwarding without matching operational efficiency leads to painful restructuring when funding tightens.

name: Shipwire
summary: Early cloud-based order fulfillment and warehousing network provider acquired by Propel in 2016, then effectively wound down by 2018 as it lost customers to newer fulfillment platforms.
lesson: Being an early mover in fulfillment tech does not protect against faster, better-funded later entrants improving on the model.

name: Otto
summary: Self-driving truck startup founded by ex-Google engineers, acquired by Uber in 2016; dissolved as a distinct unit by 2018 amid the Waymo trade-secrets lawsuit against Uber and its self-driving trucking pullback.
lesson: Acquiring technical talent under legal clouds can create massive downstream liability that ends the program entirely.

name: FreightHub
summary: Berlin-based digital freight forwarder that merged with competitor Forto in 2019 after struggling to scale independently in the crowded European freight-tech market.
lesson: Undifferentiated freight-forwarding platforms often end up as consolidation targets rather than standalone winners.

name: Rivigo
summary: Indian trucking and logistics-tech unicorn known for its relay-driver model that saw its founder-CEO ousted by the board in 2022 amid financial distress and mass layoffs, effectively ending its unicorn trajectory.
lesson: Operationally innovative logistics models can still collapse under founder-board conflict and unsustainable cash burn.
source: Economic Times, Rivigo founder ouster and layoffs 2022

name: Radial
summary: US e-commerce fulfillment and omnichannel logistics provider (formerly eBay Enterprise) acquired by Belgian postal group bpost in 2017; bpost wrote down billions in value and scaled back most US operations by 2023.
lesson: Even a large, established fulfillment operator can become a value-destroying acquisition when integration and demand assumptions miss.

name: Boxed
summary: Bulk e-commerce retailer whose Boxed Wholesale software/fulfillment arm served other retailers; went public via SPAC in 2022 and filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in 2024 after years of unprofitable growth.
lesson: A SPAC listing provides capital but not a business model fix for chronically negative unit economics.

name: Kobo360
summary: Nigerian trucking and logistics marketplace connecting shippers with truckers that underwent major restructuring and staff cuts around 2022-2023 amid funding shortages across African logistics-tech.
lesson: Emerging-market logistics marketplaces are especially exposed when global venture funding for the sector tightens.

name: Sendy
summary: Kenyan logistics and delivery-tech startup that connected businesses with transport providers, shutting down operations in 2023 after failing to secure follow-on funding.
lesson: Regional logistics platforms without deep-pocketed backers are vulnerable when venture capital retreats from the sector.

name: Lori Systems
summary: Kenyan digital freight and trucking marketplace for East African supply chains that scaled back drastically and effectively ceased core operations in 2023 amid a funding crunch.
lesson: Digitizing fragmented trucking markets is capital-intensive and highly sensitive to macro funding cycles.
source: African tech press, Lori Systems retrenchment 2023

name: Everoad
summary: French digital road-freight platform that struggled to reach profitability and was absorbed/restructured in 2019 after failing to compete against larger, better-capitalized freight-tech rivals.
lesson: A crowded freight-tech field with well-funded incumbents leaves little room for undercapitalized regional platforms.

name: Trella
summary: MENA-region digital trucking marketplace (Egypt/UAE/Saudi Arabia) that underwent significant layoffs and operational scale-back around 2023 as regional logistics-tech funding dried up.
lesson: Trucking marketplaces in emerging regions face the same commoditized-margin pressure as their US counterparts, with less capital cushion.

name: Cargo Chief
summary: US digital freight brokerage and trucking marketplace startup that wound down around 2019 after failing to differentiate its brokerage technology against larger competitors.
lesson: Freight brokerage technology alone is not a durable moat without proprietary carrier density or shipper relationships.

name: Trukkin
summary: UAE-based digital trucking and logistics marketplace that collapsed in 2023 amid allegations of financial fraud, with its founder accused of misappropriating investor and lender funds.
lesson: Founder-level financial fraud can destroy a logistics marketplace overnight regardless of its underlying commercial traction.
source: Gulf business press, Trukkin fraud collapse 2023

name: Flexport
summary: Digital freight forwarder and customs brokerage platform that grew into a multi-billion-dollar logistics company, becoming one of the most prominent supply-chain tech unicorns in the US.
lesson: Modernizing an unglamorous, fragmented industry like freight forwarding with software can create durable enterprise value.
source: Flexport funding and valuation coverage, Forbes/TechCrunch

name: project44
summary: Real-time supply-chain visibility platform connecting shippers, carriers, and logistics providers that reached unicorn status and remains a leading independent visibility provider.
lesson: Neutral, carrier-agnostic visibility platforms benefit from strong network effects across shippers and carriers.

name: FourKites
summary: Real-time supply-chain visibility platform that became a leading global provider tracking shipments across ocean, rail, and road for major enterprise shippers.
lesson: Enterprise supply-chain visibility software scales well once integrated deeply into large shippers' operational workflows.

name: Samsara
summary: IoT-based fleet management and connected-operations platform for trucking and logistics companies that completed a successful IPO in 2021 and continues to grow as a public company.
lesson: Hardware-plus-software platforms that solve compliance and safety pain points can achieve durable public-market success.
source: Samsara IPO prospectus and public filings, 2021

name: Motive (KeepTruckin)
summary: Fleet management and ELD compliance platform (rebranded from KeepTruckin to Motive) that reached unicorn status serving hundreds of thousands of fleets across trucking and logistics.
lesson: Regulatory mandates like ELD compliance can create a durable wedge market for a logistics software platform to expand from.

name: Locus Robotics
summary: Warehouse autonomous mobile robot (AMR) provider for e-commerce and 3PL fulfillment that reached unicorn status and is widely deployed across major retail and logistics warehouses.
lesson: Robots-as-a-service pricing aligned to warehouse throughput can scale profitably when deployment costs are managed carefully.

name: GreyOrange
summary: Warehouse robotics and fulfillment automation company (goods-to-person AMRs) that scaled globally across e-commerce and retail distribution centers and remains a leading independent player.
lesson: Warehouse robotics companies that diversify across geographies and verticals reduce dependence on any single customer segment.

name: Exotec
summary: French warehouse robotics company known for its Skypod cube-storage system that became a unicorn serving major retailers globally, including Uniqlo and Decathlon.
lesson: A differentiated hardware architecture (cube storage vs. flat AMRs) can carve out a durable competitive position in warehouse automation.

name: AutoStore
summary: Norwegian cube-storage warehouse automation pioneer that completed a successful IPO on the Oslo Stock Exchange in 2021, becoming one of the most valuable European logistics-tech companies.
lesson: A patented, licensable hardware architecture with a large installed base can command premium public-market valuations.

name: Symbotic
summary: Warehouse automation and robotics company whose flagship customer is Walmart; went public via SPAC in 2022 and has grown into a multi-billion-dollar public logistics automation company.
lesson: Landing and expanding with one massive anchor customer can validate a warehouse automation platform for broader retail adoption.
source: Symbotic public filings and Walmart partnership coverage

name: Stord
summary: Cloud supply-chain and fulfillment network platform connecting warehouses and carriers for e-commerce brands that reached unicorn status.
lesson: Asset-light network orchestration platforms can scale rapidly by aggregating existing warehouse capacity rather than owning it.

name: ShipBob
summary: E-commerce order fulfillment and warehousing network platform for direct-to-consumer brands that reached unicorn status serving thousands of merchants.
lesson: Serving the long tail of DTC brands with software-managed distributed fulfillment can build a large, durable customer base.

name: Deliverr
summary: E-commerce fulfillment network startup offering fast, affordable shipping for online sellers that was acquired by Shopify in 2022 to build its fulfillment network.
lesson: A fast-growing fulfillment network can become a highly strategic acquisition target for platforms seeking logistics infrastructure.

name: Fetch Robotics
summary: Warehouse autonomous mobile robotics company acquired by Zebra Technologies in 2021, giving Zebra a robotics platform to complement its enterprise data-capture business.
lesson: A focused AMR platform can achieve a strong strategic exit by complementing a larger enterprise hardware company's portfolio.

name: Kindred AI
summary: AI-powered robotic piece-picking company for warehouse fulfillment acquired by UK grocery-tech company Ocado in 2020 to bolster its automated fulfillment centers.
lesson: Deep AI/robotics picking expertise can command a strong exit when a grocery-automation leader needs the capability quickly.

name: Roadie
funding: Acquired by UPS for undisclosed sum
summary: Crowdsourced local same-day delivery network acquired by UPS in 2021 to expand its last-mile and same-day delivery capabilities.
lesson: Crowdsourced delivery networks can find a strong home inside a legacy carrier looking to add flexible last-mile capacity.

name: Coyote Logistics
summary: Technology-enabled truckload freight brokerage acquired by UPS in 2015, becoming a cornerstone of UPS's freight brokerage business.
lesson: A tech-enabled freight brokerage with strong carrier relationships and data can command a premium acquisition price from legacy logistics players.

name: Llamasoft
summary: Supply-chain design and digital-twin simulation software company acquired by Coupa Software in 2020, becoming a key part of Coupa's business-spend-management suite.
lesson: Deep analytical supply-chain design software can command a premium exit when integrated into a broader spend-management platform.

name: Freightos
summary: International freight booking and rate-comparison marketplace (air, ocean, and land) that completed a Nasdaq IPO in 2023, becoming a publicly traded global freight tech platform.
lesson: Digitizing freight rate comparison across modes can build a durable marketplace that eventually supports a public listing.

name: Sennder
summary: European digital road-freight forwarder that reached unicorn status by digitizing trucking capacity across Europe and remains a leading independent freight-tech company.
lesson: Digital freight forwarding can succeed at scale when paired with disciplined carrier-network expansion across fragmented regional markets.

name: Interos
summary: Supply-chain risk-mapping and resilience intelligence platform that reached unicorn status, helping enterprises and governments monitor multi-tier supplier risk.
lesson: Global supply-chain shocks (COVID, trade wars) created durable enterprise demand for supplier-risk visibility software.

name: Turvo
summary: Collaborative transportation-management and supply-chain platform used by shippers, brokers, and carriers that has grown into a widely adopted enterprise logistics software provider.
lesson: A collaboration-first platform connecting multiple logistics stakeholders can outlast single-sided marketplace competitors.

name: Zipline
summary: Autonomous drone logistics company delivering medical supplies and retail goods that has scaled operations across multiple countries and reached unicorn valuation.
lesson: Focusing initial drone-delivery deployment on high-value, life-critical cargo (medical supplies) built credibility before broader commercial expansion.

name: Kodiak Robotics
summary: Autonomous long-haul trucking company that completed a public listing via SPAC in 2025, focused on driverless freight lanes and defense applications.
lesson: Focusing autonomous trucking on well-defined highway routes rather than full point-to-point autonomy improved the path to commercialization.

name: Aurora Innovation
summary: Autonomous vehicle and trucking technology company that went public via SPAC in 2021 and launched commercial driverless freight operations on Texas highways.
lesson: Sustained, well-capitalized R&D investment in autonomy can eventually reach commercial driverless deployment where less-funded rivals failed.
source: Aurora Innovation public filings and driverless launch coverage

name: Blume Global
summary: Multimodal transportation and supply-chain execution platform acquired by E2open in 2021 to expand its logistics execution and visibility offerings.
lesson: Legacy logistics execution platforms with deep carrier integrations remain valuable acquisition targets for supply-chain software consolidators.

name: Xeneta
summary: Ocean and air freight rate benchmarking and market-intelligence platform that became the industry standard reference for freight rate data among shippers and forwarders.
lesson: Aggregating proprietary rate data from many shippers creates a network-effect data moat that is hard for new entrants to replicate.

name: Onfleet
summary: Last-mile delivery route optimization and driver-management SaaS platform serving thousands of businesses that grew profitably with modest venture funding.
lesson: A capital-efficient SaaS approach can build a durable, profitable logistics software business without chasing unicorn-scale funding.
source: Onfleet company profile and funding history

name: Curri
summary: On-demand delivery logistics platform focused on the construction and industrial materials supply chain that scaled into a well-funded, growing logistics-tech company.
lesson: Targeting an underserved, unglamorous vertical (construction supply delivery) avoided direct competition with consumer delivery giants.

name: Trucker Path
funding: Acquired by Trimble for undisclosed sum
summary: Trucking navigation, parking, and load-board mobile app with millions of truck-driver users that was acquired by Trimble Transportation in 2022 to expand its driver-facing offerings.
lesson: Building a large, loyal driver user base around daily-utility features can translate into a strong strategic acquisition.

name: Parade
funding: Acquired by Trimble for undisclosed sum
summary: Freight capacity-management software helping brokers automate carrier sourcing that was acquired by Trimble Transportation in 2023 to bolster its freight-tech portfolio.
lesson: Narrow, high-value automation tools for freight brokers can be attractive tuck-in acquisitions for larger transportation software suites.

name: uShip
summary: Online freight and shipping marketplace connecting shippers with independent carriers that was acquired by trucking company ArcBest in 2021 after nearly two decades of independent operation.
lesson: Long-running two-sided freight marketplaces can remain relevant for decades before being folded into a larger logistics operator.

name: ClearMetal
summary: Supply-chain visibility and predictive analytics startup acquired by project44 in 2021 to strengthen project44's inventory and shipment visibility capabilities.
lesson: Specialized predictive-analytics capabilities can be valuable tuck-in acquisitions for larger visibility platforms consolidating the market.

name: Zencargo
summary: UK-based digital freight forwarder and supply-chain platform that scaled across European trade lanes, raising significant venture funding to grow its forwarding and visibility offering.
lesson: Combining freight forwarding services with proprietary visibility software differentiates against pure digital-forwarder competitors.

name: Bringg
summary: Delivery orchestration and last-mile logistics platform used by major retailers that was acquired by telecom group e& (Etisalat) in 2024 after a decade of independent growth.
lesson: Delivery orchestration middleware connecting retailers to many carriers can build enough enterprise value for a strong strategic exit.

name: Kiva Systems
summary: Pioneering warehouse robotics company (shelf-moving AMRs) acquired by Amazon in 2012, becoming the foundation of Amazon Robotics and reshaping warehouse automation industry-wide.
lesson: A genuinely novel warehouse automation architecture can become foundational infrastructure for the world's largest e-commerce operator.

name: Delhivery
summary: India's largest third-party logistics and supply-chain services company, covering last-mile delivery, warehousing, and freight, that completed a successful IPO on India's National Stock Exchange in 2022.
lesson: Building an integrated full-stack logistics network across a large, fragmented market can culminate in a durable public-market listing.

name: J&T Express
summary: Southeast Asian and Chinese express-delivery and logistics network that expanded rapidly across emerging markets and completed a Hong Kong IPO in 2023.
lesson: Aggressive but well-financed geographic expansion into underserved delivery markets can culminate in a successful public listing.
source: J&T Express Hong Kong IPO coverage, 2023

name: BlackBuck
summary: Indian trucking marketplace connecting shippers with truckers that reached unicorn status and remains one of India's largest digital freight platforms.
lesson: Digitizing a massive, highly fragmented trucking market can create durable scale advantages once density is reached.
